declaration.reason: The synthetic Phase 0 fixture remains incomparable with the global brownfield baseline. Same-catalog shadow gates pass for the authorized Landing and Internal Dashboard apps, while Turbo remains outside scope.

validation.note: The absolute microsecond gate and relative end-to-end percentage gate are reported independently; a failed relative gate is not hidden by the renderer decision.

moduleInitialization.scope: first import is timed inside a fresh Node process; reevaluation uses unique-query ESM imports after runtime and memory measurements
first import is timed inside a fresh Node process; reevaluation uses unique-query ESM imports after runtime and memory measurements
first import is timed inside a fresh Node process; reevaluation uses unique-query ESM imports after runtime and memory measurements

representationSelection.selectedBecause: Bundle deltas within the configured byte tolerance and runtime distributions within the configured percentage tolerance are treated as ties; generated artifact gzip resolves the remaining tie.

candidates.reason: The actual i18next-icu TFunction bridge cannot produce the required rich component output and fails with INTL_RENDERER_FAILURE.

compatibility.supportPolicy: Only complete, exact correlated tuples can be supported. Semver peer ranges that admit untested cross-products are forbidden.

rows.constraint: No i18next or React peer is permitted on the framework-free Node renderer.

rows.note: Convention discovery, AST-inferred contracts, declaration, parity, and bundle gates pass, and the accepted leaf uses one generic i18next-reactive runtime through local links. This local evidence does not qualify the full renderer tuple or authorize release, publication, or platform-wide migration.

Convention discovery, AST-inferred contracts, declaration, parity, and bundle gates pass, and the accepted leaf uses one generic i18next-reactive runtime through local links. This local evidence does not qualify the full renderer tuple or authorize release, publication, or platform-wide migration.

compatibility.publicationRule: The core ABI/compiler/runtime packages publish no broad i18next or React peer promise. A future framework adapter may publish only exact qualified tuple disjunctions and must also enforce the generated renderer-stack hash at registration.

rationale: precompiled-v1 is the only candidate with full text, value, and rich-message conformance.
precompiled is the only evidence-supported representation for backend-specific precompiled message functions; constants and proxy require normalized-IR fallback.
The full rich-capable catalog is backend-specific because the TFunction bridge does not satisfy rich-output parity.
Comparable declaration, TypeScript, bundle, and exact-parity gates pass for the authorized Landing and Internal Dashboard shadow catalogs.
Turbo remains outside the authorized application implementation scope, so the global every-catalog gate remains false.
The preliminary relative validation-cost gate remains recorded separately from the absolute runtime budget.
Phase 0 evidence does not authorize application migration.

fe-mirai-internal-dashboard.note: The committed HEAD is pinned here; the shadow report separately hashes the Phase 0 worktree inputs and records its dirty state.

baselineAudit.note: These are immutable initial-audit counts, not the current diagnostics for the authorized shadow catalogs.

fe-mirai-internal-dashboard.repairs: Six Thai argument-role mismatches were repaired with regression coverage.

fe-openmirai-landing.repairs: The audited ICU and Mustache source defects were repaired with regression coverage.

localeFindings.policy: Compiler errors remain strict. Baseline defects are preserved as audit evidence; authorized shadow repairs are explicit source changes and are never normalized or accepted silently.

identical.migrationPrecondition: Remove learner duplicates only after the generated provenance report proves byte-identical EN and TH values at the pinned source commits.

differing.replacementRule: A future learner wording change requires one exact-key replacement declaration containing the shared fragment ID, exact version, exact content hash, reason, owner, and ADR provenance. Prefix and wildcard replacements are forbidden.
differing.migrationPrecondition: Confirm product intent against the current built Learner strings. Until then, the shared values remain authoritative and learner duplicates are non-authoritative.

learnerOnly.migrationPrecondition: Mount these leaves explicitly under the learner fragment and prove that composition no longer replaces the containing branch. Restoring a previously unreachable key is not permission to change any rendered wording or call site.

e2eDivergence.policy: E2E may consume only the exact packed catalog and manifest selected by the deployed build after the Phase 0 gate; sibling source crawling is not authoritative.

allowed: one generic convention runtime and accepted local non-authoritative leaf in Landing and Internal Dashboard
forbiddenUntilLaterPhase: production or app-wide application consumer migration
phaseBoundary.pilotAuthority: The two generic local runtimes and accepted leaves are explicit user-authorized implementation evidence only. Pilot flags and feature-specific runtimes are removed; migrationAuthorized, everyCatalogGateReady, and application authoritative flags remain false.

fixedIterationMemory.note: Negative retained-heap deltas remain noisy supporting evidence and cannot freeze an allocation budget.
Negative retained-heap deltas remain noisy supporting evidence and cannot freeze an allocation budget.

proxyOneByteGzipEdgeAssessment.note: The proxy gzip edge is inside the practical byte tie, but the measured primary timing evidence does not independently establish that constants outweigh it; the ADR must use semantic eligibility and the full evidence set.

evidence.tsserverNote: The proxy uses the same TypeScript language-service engine as tsserver. TypeScript 7.0.2 has no tsserver binary, so only compiler fixtures are available for that lane.
